compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Summary
tests/integration/test_store_concurrency.py::test_concurrent_writer_storm_no_loss (integration tier, run under pytest-xdist -n4 on GitHub-hosted runners) intermittently fails when its 8 concurrent subprocess writers race the git object/tree build on the shared store. Two distinct symptoms observed, same root class:
- 'git commit failed while holding lock: error: invalid object 100644 <sha> for "<ticket>/...-COMMENT.json" ... error: Error building trees' (run 29300833741, main f542/560180461, ubuntu py3.12, 2026-07-14)
- 'git commit failed while holding lock: fatal: could not parse HEAD' (run 29296542560, main 5348d2adf, integration tier, 2026-07-13)

## Reproduction Steps
1. Run the integration tier under load: pytest -m integration -n 4 (as CI does) on a loaded runner.
2. Intermittently: all 8 writers in test_concurrent_writer_storm_no_loss fail with a git tree-build/HEAD-parse error while holding the store lock.
Expected: concurrent writers serialize cleanly (the test asserts no writer failures + no lost events).
Actual: under contention a writer builds a tree referencing a blob object not yet durable / reads an unborn-or-mid-update HEAD, and git aborts with 'invalid object ... Error building trees' or 'could not parse HEAD'.
Passes 5/5 locally on an unloaded machine — load-dependent.

## Notes
Not introduced by recent changes (observed on both 5348d2adf and f542). Distinct from the b228 timing flake and the 85c3 harness/acli flakes. Likely a git object-write vs tree-build ordering race in the store write path under the lock (concurrent processes sharing one git dir).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] test_concurrent_writer_storm_no_loss no longer fails under CI-load parallelism (the git tree-build/HEAD race is closed — e.g. ensure blobs are durably written before tree-build, or serialize the object-write+commit under the lock, or retry on the transient object/ref error).
- [ ] It still asserts no lost events under a concurrent writer storm.
- [ ] Regression coverage runs in the gerrit-verify Verified gate (integration tier).
compiled_state.title: Flaky integration test test_concurrent_writer_storm_no_loss — concurrent git tree-build race under CI load
